frameworks.name: good-based model of economic choice
frameworks.one_line: values computed per good and compared in goods space, independent of sensorimotor contingencies

frameworks.name: subjective value integration
frameworks.one_line: multi-dimensional option attributes integrated into a single subjective value at choice time

frameworks.name: transitivity of preference
frameworks.one_line: independent good-by-good value computation implies transitive choice

frameworks.name: action-based / sensorimotor decision models
frameworks.one_line: alternative view where value computation is tied to motor planning

core_question: At what level of representation and in which brain regions does the neural comparison of values underlying economic choice take place?
key_assumptions: options vary along multiple dimensions integrated into a single subjective value
values are computed at the time of choice rather than learned as such
values of different goods are computed independently of one another
decisions reduce to comparisons of these subjective values
manipulations: review across neurophysiology, imaging, and lesion paradigms
key_findings: abstract value representations exist in orbitofrontal cortex
abstract value representations exist in ventromedial prefrontal cortex
value signals are framed independently of the sensorimotor contingencies of choice
interpretations: value computation and comparison plausibly take place within orbitofrontal and ventromedial prefrontal cortices
economic choice is best understood as comparison within the space of goods rather than within motor space
